meaning: n.三位一体,三人一个组,三个一组的(物)
examples: At the church of the Trinity in Cherbourg on Christmas Day, 1892, he had his debut as a composer with the performance of his andante for string trio and organ.
In 1923, he moved on to Trinity College Dublin.
Or was Carradine No. 3 in a previous trinity of death?
Adam Byatt's chic Trinity Restaurant in Clapham is perfect for an evening out with friends.
Founded in 1592, Trinity was for centuries a bastion of Protestant ascendancy in Ireland.
